capabilities: Integrate subprocess invocation safety measures with architectural best practices to enhance reliability and traceability in CLI agent operations.

procedure.action: Invoke subprocesses using a controlled environment by setting resource limits with ulimit and timeout commands to prevent resource exhaustion and ensure timely termination using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Capture and log subprocess stdout and stderr streams using tee command to a centralized logging system, ensuring all outputs are traceable and auditable using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Assert subprocess exit codes by checking against a predefined list of acceptable codes; trigger alerting mechanisms if unexpected codes are encountered by asserting all required fields conform to the schema definition; emit VALIDATION_FAILED with field path on the first failing field; assert field_validation_pass_rate >= 1.0.

Reconcile subprocess outputs by applying JSON Schema validation on the combined output; log validation results and discrepancies to a structured audit trail by applying field-level structural comparison on shared key fields; flag conflicts where hash(A.field) != hash(B.field) and log to conflict_log; assert merge_error_count < 1.

Invoke retry logic with exponential backoff using a retry library to handle transient subprocess failures, ensuring robustness without overwhelming the system following the specified pattern; write a unit test covering the primary code path; assert test_coverage >= 0.8 for the modified module before marking complete.

summary: Ensure safe and efficient subprocess invocation for CLI agents by merging insights from software architecture and agent execution knowledge bases.
title: Safe Subprocess Invocation for CLI Agents

failureConditions: Resource exhaustion occurs when subprocesses run without limits, causing system instability and potential crashes.
Loss of traceability when subprocess outputs are not logged, leading to difficulties in auditing and debugging.
Unexpected subprocess exit codes cause unhandled exceptions, leading to workflow disruptions and potential data loss.
metrics: subprocess exit codes by checking against a predefined list of acceptable codes
test_coverage >= 0.8 for the modified module before marking complete
successConditions: subprocess_exit_code in [0, 1, 2]; assert error_rate < 0.001 and operation_completed = true.
